verses.latin_vulgate: Ego sum vitis vera, et Pater meus agricola est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγώ εἰμι ἡ ἄμπελος ἡ ἀληθινή καὶ ὁ πατήρ μου ὁ γεωργός ἐστιν
verses.king_james: I am the true vine, and my Father is the husbandman.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnem palmitem in me non ferentem fructum, tollet eum, et omnem qui fert fructum, purgabit eum, ut fructum plus afferat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶν κλῆμα ἐν ἐμοὶ μὴ φέρον καρπόν αἴρει αὐτό καὶ πᾶν τὸ καρπὸν φέρον καθαίρει αὐτὸ ἵνα πλείονα καρπὸν φέρῃ
verses.king_james: Every branch in me that beareth not fruit he taketh away: and every *branch* that beareth fruit, he purgeth it, that it may bring forth more fruit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jam vos mundi estis propter sermonem quem locutus sum vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἤδη ὑμεῖς καθαροί ἐστε διὰ τὸν λόγον ὃν λελάληκα ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: Now ye are clean through the word which I have spoken unto you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Manete in me, et ego in vobis. Sicut palmes non potest fere fructum a semetipso, nisi manserit in vite, sic nec vos, nisi in me manseritis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μείνατε ἐν ἐμοί κἀγὼ ἐν ὑμῖν καθὼς τὸ κλῆμα οὐ δύναται καρπὸν φέρειν ἀφ᾽ ἑαυτοῦ ἐὰν μὴ μείνῃ ἐν τῇ ἀμπέλῳ οὕτως οὐδὲ ὑμεῖς ἐὰν μὴ ἐν ἐμοὶ μείνητε
verses.king_james: Abide in me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine; no more can ye, except ye abide in me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego sum vitis, vos palmites: qui manet in me, et ego in eo, hic fert fructum multum, quia sine me nihil potestis facere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγώ εἰμι ἡ ἄμπελος ὑμεῖς τὰ κλήματα ὁ μένων ἐν ἐμοὶ κἀγὼ ἐν αὐτῷ οὗτος φέρει καρπὸν πολύν ὅτι χωρὶς ἐμοῦ οὐ δύνασθε ποιεῖν οὐδέν
verses.king_james: I am the vine, ye *are* the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: for without me ye can do nothing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si quis in me non manserit, mittetur foras sicut palmes, et arescet, et colligent eum, et in ignem mittent, et ardet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν μή τις μείνῃ ἐν ἐμοί ἐβλήθη ἔξω ὡς τὸ κλῆμα καὶ ἐξηράνθη καὶ συνάγουσιν αὐτὰ καὶ εἰς πῦρ βάλλουσιν καὶ καίεται
verses.king_james: If a man abide not in me, he is cast forth as a branch, and is withered; and men gather them, and cast *them* into the fire, and they are burned.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si manseritis in me, et verba mea in vobis manserint, quodcumque volueritis petetis, et fiet vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν μείνητε ἐν ἐμοὶ καὶ τὰ ῥήματά μου ἐν ὑμῖν μείνῃ ὃ ἐὰν θέλητε αἰτήσεσθε καὶ γενήσεται ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: If ye abide in me, and my words abide in you, ye shall ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you.

verses.latin_vulgate: In hoc clarificatus est Pater meus, ut fructum plurimum afferatis, et efficiamini mei discipuli.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν τούτῳ ἐδοξάσθη ὁ πατήρ μου ἵνα καρπὸν πολὺν φέρητε καὶ γενήσεσθε ἐμοὶ μαθηταί
verses.king_james: Herein is my Father glorified, that ye bear much fruit; so shall ye be my disciples.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut dilexit me Pater, et ego dilexi vos. Manete in dilectione mea.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθὼς ἠγάπησέν με ὁ πατήρ κἀγὼ ἠγάπησα ὑμᾶς μείνατε ἐν τῇ ἀγάπῃ τῇ ἐμῇ
verses.king_james: As the Father hath loved me, so have I loved you: continue ye in my love.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si præcepta mea servaveritis, manebitis in dilectione mea, sicut et ego Patris mei præcepta servavi, et maneo in ejus dilectione.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐὰν τὰς ἐντολάς μου τηρήσητε μενεῖτε ἐν τῇ ἀγάπῃ μου καθὼς ἐγὼ τὰς ἐντολὰς τοῦ πατρός μου τετήρηκα καὶ μένω αὐτοῦ ἐν τῇ ἀγάπῃ
verses.king_james: If ye keep my commandments, ye shall abide in my love; even as I have kept my Father's commandments, and abide in his love.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc locutus sum vobis: ut gaudium meum in vobis sit, et gaudium vestrum impleatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα λελάληκα ὑμῖν ἵνα ἡ χαρὰ ἡ ἐμὴ ἐν ὑμῖν μείνῃ, καὶ ᾖ χαρὰ ὑμῶν πληρωθῇ
verses.king_james: These things have I spoken unto you, that my joy might remain in you, and *that* your joy might be full.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc est præceptum meum, ut diligatis invicem, sicut dilexi vos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αὕτη ἐστὶν ἡ ἐντολὴ ἡ ἐμή ἵνα ἀγαπᾶτε ἀλλήλους καθὼς ἠγάπησα ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: This is my commandment, That ye love one another, as I have loved you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Majorem hac dilectionem nemo habet, ut animam suam ponat qui pro amicis suis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μείζονα ταύτης ἀγάπην οὐδεὶς ἔχει ἵνα τις τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ θῇ ὑπὲρ τῶν φίλων αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vos amici mei estis, si feceritis quæ ego præcipio vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμεῖς φίλοι μού ἐστε ἐὰν ποιῆτε ὅσα ἐγὼ ἐντέλλομαι ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: Ye are my friends, if ye do whatsoever I command you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jam non dicam vos servos: quia servus nescit quid faciat dominus ejus. Vos autem dixi amicos: quia omnia quæcumque audivi a Patre meo, nota feci vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκέτι ὑμᾶς λέγω δούλους ὅτι ὁ δοῦλος οὐκ οἶδεν τί ποιεῖ αὐτοῦ ὁ κύριος ὑμᾶς δὲ εἴρηκα φίλους ὅτι πάντα ἃ ἤκουσα παρὰ τοῦ πατρός μου ἐγνώρισα ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: Henceforth I call you not servants; for the servant knoweth not what his lord doeth: but I have called you friends; for all things that I have heard of my Father I have made known unto you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non vos me elegistis, sed ego elegi vos, et posui vos ut eatis, et fructum afferatis, et fructus vester maneat: ut quodcumque petieritis Patrem in nomine meo, det vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐχ ὑμεῖς με ἐξελέξασθε ἀλλ᾽ ἐγὼ ἐξελεξάμην ὑμᾶς καὶ ἔθηκα ὑμᾶς ἵνα ὑμεῖς ὑπάγητε καὶ καρπὸν φέρητε καὶ ὁ καρπὸς ὑμῶν μένῃ ἵνα ὅ τι ἂν αἰτήσητε τὸν πατέρα ἐν τῷ ὀνόματί μου δῷ ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: Ye have not chosen me, but I have chosen you, and ordained you, that ye should go and bring forth fruit, and *that* your fruit should remain: that whatsoever ye shall ask of the Father in my name, he may give it you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc mando vobis: ut diligatis invicem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα ἐντέλλομαι ὑμῖν ἵνα ἀγαπᾶτε ἀλλήλους
verses.king_james: These things I command you, that ye love one another.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si mundus vos odit, scitote quia me priorem vobis odio habuit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ ὁ κόσμος ὑμᾶς μισεῖ γινώσκετε ὅτι ἐμὲ πρῶτον ὑμῶν μεμίσηκεν
verses.king_james: If the world hate you, ye know that it hated me before *it hated* you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si de mundo fuissetis, mundus quod suum erat diligeret: quia vero de mundo non estis, sed ego elegi vos de mundo, propterea odit vos mundus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ ἐκ τοῦ κόσμου ἦτε ὁ κόσμος ἂν τὸ ἴδιον ἐφίλει ὅτι δὲ ἐκ τοῦ κόσμου οὐκ ἐστέ ἀλλ᾽ ἐγὼ ἐξελεξάμην ὑμᾶς ἐκ τοῦ κόσμου διὰ τοῦτο μισεῖ ὑμᾶς ὁ κόσμος
verses.king_james: If ye were of the world, the world would love his own: but because ye are not of the world, but I have chosen you out of the world, therefore the world hateth you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mementote sermonis mei, quem ego dixi vobis: non est servus major domino suo. Si me persecuti sunt, et vos persequentur; si sermonem meum servaverunt, et vestrum servabunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μνημονεύετε τοῦ λόγου οὗ ἐγὼ εἶπον ὑμῖν οὐκ ἔστιν δοῦλος μείζων τοῦ κυρίου αὐτοῦ εἰ ἐμὲ ἐδίωξαν καὶ ὑμᾶς διώξουσιν εἰ τὸν λόγον μου ἐτήρησαν καὶ τὸν ὑμέτερον τηρήσουσιν
verses.king_james: Remember the word that I said unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord. If they have persecuted me, they will also persecute you; if they have kept my saying, they will keep yours also.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed hæc omnia facient vobis propter nomen meum: quia nesciunt eum qui misit me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ ταῦτα πάντα ποιήσουσιν ὑμῖν διὰ τὸ ὄνομά μου ὅτι οὐκ οἴδασιν τὸν πέμψαντά με
verses.king_james: But all these things will they do unto you for my name's sake, because they know not him that sent me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si non venissem, et locutus fuissem eis, peccatum non haberent: nunc autem excusationem non habent de peccato suo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ μὴ ἦλθον καὶ ἐλάλησα αὐτοῖς ἁμαρτίαν οὐκ εἴχον νῦν δὲ πρόφασιν οὐκ ἔχουσιν περὶ τῆς ἁμαρτίας αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: If I had not come and spoken unto them, they had not had sin: but now they have no cloke for their sin.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui me odit, et Patrem meum odit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ ἐμὲ μισῶν καὶ τὸν πατέρα μου μισεῖ
verses.king_james: He that hateth me hateth my Father also.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si opera non fecissem in eis quæ nemo alius fecit, peccatum non haberent: nunc autem et viderunt, et oderunt et me, et Patrem meum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ τὰ ἔργα μὴ ἐποίησα ἐν αὐτοῖς ἃ οὐδεὶς ἄλλος πεποίηκεν, ἁμαρτίαν οὐκ εἴχον νῦν δὲ καὶ ἑωράκασιν καὶ μεμισήκασιν καὶ ἐμὲ καὶ τὸν πατέρα μου
verses.king_james: If I had not done among them the works which none other man did, they had not had sin: but now have they both seen and hated both me and my Father.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed ut adimpleatur sermo, qui in lege eorum scriptus est: Quia odio habuerunt me gratis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ἵνα πληρωθῇ ὁ λόγος ὁ γεγραμμένος ἐν τῷ νόμῳ αὐτῶν ὅτι ἐμίσησάν με δωρεάν
verses.king_james: But *this cometh to pass*, that the word might be fulfilled that is written in their law, They hated me without a cause.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem venerit Paraclitus, quem ego mittam vobis a Patre, Spiritum veritatis, qui a Patre procedit, ille testimonium perhibebit de me;
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν δὲ ἔλθῃ ὁ παράκλητος ὃν ἐγὼ πέμψω ὑμῖν παρὰ τοῦ πατρός τὸ πνεῦμα τῆς ἀληθείας ὃ παρὰ τοῦ πατρὸς ἐκπορεύεται ἐκεῖνος μαρτυρήσει περὶ ἐμοῦ
verses.king_james: But when the Comforter is come, whom I will send unto you from the Father, *even* the Spirit of truth, which proceedeth from the Father, he shall testify of me:

verses.latin_vulgate: et vos testimonium perhibebitis, quia ab initio mecum estis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὑμεῖς δὲ μαρτυρεῖτε ὅτι ἀπ᾽ ἀρχῆς μετ᾽ ἐμοῦ ἐστε
verses.king_james: And ye also shall bear witness, because ye have been with me from the beginning.
